extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won the sprint by narrowest margin over JAKOBSEN, with BORA-hansgrohe delivering textbook leadout in treacherous wet conditions.
tactical_patterns: LOTTO NL-Jumbo controlled break on short leash showing Groenewegen sprint train preparation, keeping gap manageable entire stage.
Five-rider break with two TT specialists (Dowsett, Grivko) still caught, suggesting teams prioritized sprint over time gaps despite conditions.

rider_signals.GRIVKO Andriy: positive: second consecutive day in breakaway showing aggressive racing form despite veteran status
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remi: positive: contested both intermediate sprints successfully from break, hunting bonus seconds suggesting GC ambitions for later mountain stage
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: vanquished by Ackermann despite holding red jersey, suggesting sprint form inferior to German champion
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: DNF on stage 2 ending BMC career prematurely, significant retirement signal
